task_completion.description: Open bcWebCam and navigate to its barcode settings/options.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the bcWebCam application running with its settings, preferences, or options dialog open.

task_completion.description: Disable 'QR Code' scanning in bcWebCam settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the bcWebCam settings showing the 'QR Code' option unchecked or disabled.

task_completion.description: Ensure 'EAN-13' scanning is enabled in bcWebCam settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the bcWebCam settings showing the 'EAN-13' option checked or enabled.
Screenshot showing the bcWebCam main window with the settings dialog no longer visible, or the application closed entirely.

task_completion.description: Look up the product barcode in the provided CSV database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the file 'openfoodfacts_beverages.csv' open in a spreadsheet viewer, text editor, or being queried via a terminal command.

task_completion.description: Create a text file at 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\product_report.txt' with the extracted product information.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing a text editor with 'product_report.txt' open, or a terminal displaying its contents, formatted exactly with 'Barcode:', 'Product:', 'Brand:', and 'Nutriscore:' lines.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the bcWebCam GUI to change settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual bcWebCam application interface being interacted with, not terminal commands modifying hidden configuration files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually looked up the data in the CSV file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the CSV file open or a script/command querying it, proving the agent didn't hallucinate or use external knowledge for the product details.

integrity.description: Verify the output file is saved in the correct location.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor title bar, file explorer, or terminal should show the file path explicitly as 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\product_report.txt'.
